title: Protection of newborn animals through maternal immunization.
ehr_evidence: None mentioned; the manuscript focuses on veterinary medicine and maternal immunization in animals.
summary: The manuscript provides an overview of maternal vaccination as a method to provide passive immunity to neonatal animals, discussing placental transport mechanisms and specific examples in cattle, dogs, and pigs.
key_findings: Maternal vaccination is a successful approach for providing early life protection against infectious diseases in animals.
Passive immunity is transferred to neonates via the placenta or colostrum.
Vaccines for calf diarrhoea are well-established and safe in the field.
captured_features: Antibody titers in colostrum and milk
